segments.text: First, listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Why was the ambassador particularly lucky?

translations.zh-Hans: 这位大使为什么格外幸运？

segments.text: When the ambassador of Escalopia returned home for lunch, his wife got a shock.

translations.zh-Hans: 埃斯卡洛皮亚大使回家吃午饭时，他的妻子吓了一跳。

segments.text: He looked pale, and his clothes were in a frightful state.

translations.zh-Hans: 他脸色苍白，衣服也狼狈不堪。

segments.text: What has happened?
she asked.
How did your clothes get into such a mess?
A fire extinguisher, my dear, answered the ambassador dryly.

translations.zh-Hans: “灭火器，亲爱的。”大使冷冷地回答。

segments.text: University students set the embassy on fire this morning.

translations.zh-Hans: “大学生今天上午放火烧了大使馆。”

segments.text: Good heavens!
exclaimed his wife.
And where were you at the time?
I was in my office as usual, answered the ambassador.

translations.zh-Hans: “我像往常一样在办公室里。”大使回答。

segments.text: The fire broke out in the basement.
I went down immediately, of course, and that fool, Horst, aimed a fire extinguisher at me.

translations.zh-Hans: “我当然立刻下楼，那个叫霍斯特的傻瓜却拿灭火器对准了我。”

segments.text: He thought I was on fire.
I must definitely get that fellow posted.
The ambassador's wife went on asking questions when she suddenly noticed a big hole in her husband's hat.

translations.zh-Hans: 大使夫人继续追问，突然发现丈夫的帽子上有一个大洞。

segments.text: And how can you explain that?
she asked.
Oh, that, said the ambassador.
Someone fired a shot through my office window.

translations.zh-Hans: “有人朝我的办公室窗户开了一枪。”

segments.text: Accurate, don't you think?
Fortunately, I wasn't wearing it at the time.

translations.zh-Hans: “幸运的是，当时我没戴这顶帽子。”

segments.text: If I had been, I would not have been able to get home for lunch.

translations.zh-Hans: “如果戴着，我就没法回家吃午饭了。”
